{"step_by_step_thinking": "The Rb gene, also called the 'Governor of the Cell Cycle,' encodes the RB protein that, when hypophosphorylated, binds and inhibits E2F transcription factors. These transcription factors regulate genes essential for cells to pass from G1 to the S phase of the cell cycle. When the Rb gene phosphorylation is defective, the RB protein cannot inhibit E2F, leading to unchecked cell cycle progression and division.", "answer_choice": "C"}
